values.value:
== PUBLIC GROUP RULES == File path: groups/GroupApiName.group metadataType: Group memberName: GroupApiName
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <Group xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <name>Group Display Name</name> <doesIncludeBosses>false</doesIncludeBosses> <members> <members>username@example.com</members> <type>User</type> </members> <members> <members>CEO</members> <type>Role</type> </members> </Group>
MANDATORY ELEMENT ORDER: 1. name (required — display label, spaces allowed) 2. doesIncludeBosses (optional — defaults to false) 3. members (optional — one block per member, repeating)
VALID ELEMENTS — ONLY these three exist on Group metadata: <name>, <doesIncludeBosses>, <members> NOTHING ELSE. Do not add any other elements.
STRICTLY FORBIDDEN — these do NOT exist on Group: <doesSendEmailToMembers> ← Queue-only, NOT valid on Group <type>Regular</type> ← NOT a valid Group element <sharedTo> ← does not exist in Group metadata
MEMBER TYPES — <type> value inside each <members> block: User → <members> = username (email address) Role → <members> = exact role name (e.g. CEO) RoleAndSubordinates → <members> = role name, includes all subordinates Group → <members> = DeveloperName of another public group AllInternalUsers → <members> = AllInternalUsers
RULES: - GroupApiName (DeveloperName) has no spaces — use underscores - For UPDATES: regenerate complete XML preserving existing members, only change what ticket asks - For DELETE: use destructiveFiles with metadataType Group and member = GroupApiName - Never use Group metadata for Queues — Queues have their own metadata type
Public Group XML format and member type rules
